steps.action: Raas or Dandiya Raas is the socio-religious folk with weight transfer through footwork, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Raas or Dandiya Raas is the socio-religious folk with weight transfer through footwork, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: The dance is performed in the Marwar region of R
steps.text: The dance is performed in the Marwar region of Rajasthan too.

steps.action: The dance style is now in a competitive format a
steps.text: The dance style is now in a competitive format and a traditional format.

steps.action: Men and women traditionally play dandiya-raas an
steps.text: Men and women traditionally play dandiya-raas and the dance operates in pairs, meaning the group must contain an even number.

steps.action: Generally, two lines are formed, with partners f
steps.text: Generally, two lines are formed, with partners facing each other:The lines move clockwise, and each person steps forward to hit sticks with their partner, then moves on two people.

steps.action: At the end of the line, each turns and joins the
steps.text: At the end of the line, each turns and joins the line opposite, so the movement is continuous.

steps.action: Each one then turns away to the left to hit thei
steps.text: Each one then turns away to the left to hit their own sticks together before turning back to the partner to hit the right sticks again, and before moving on two places to a new partner.

steps.action: In Saurashtra, Raas is performed by the men and
steps.text: In Saurashtra, Raas is performed by the men and the dance performed by women is called as Raasda.

steps.action: Element of dance is more prominent in the Raas w
steps.text: Element of dance is more prominent in the Raas while music is more prominent in Raasda.

steps.action: Garba-Raas emerged as a competitive dance style
steps.text: Garba-Raas emerged as a competitive dance style due to the efforts of the Federation of Gujarati Associations of North America (FOGNA).

steps.action: The Garba-Raas competitive dance style was furth
steps.text: The Garba-Raas competitive dance style was further developed in the early 2000s by first generation Indian-American college students.

steps.action: competitive sport, rather than an inclusive, rit
steps.text: competitive sport, rather than an inclusive, ritualistic dance form, the need to "entertain" has become increasingly paramount” by Falcone.

steps.action: In order to turn this social dance into a compet
steps.text: In order to turn this social dance into a competitive activity, many aspects of the dance, such as the formations, the moves, and structures, have changed.

steps.action: The new evolved style is described in further de
steps.text: The new evolved style is described in further detail below under “ The Garba-Raas Competitive Dance Style”.

steps.action: Similarities between the traditional and competi
steps.text: Similarities between the traditional and competitive styles are the clothing, the music, and the basic moves.

steps.action: Jessica Falcone points out that dancers prefer t
steps.text: Jessica Falcone points out that dancers prefer to identify the new dance style as a branch of Garba and Dandiya-raas but in reality, she states that intercollegiate Garba-Raas is transforming into its own dance style.

steps.action: The structure competition dance consists of 12-1
steps.text: The structure competition dance consists of 12-16 dancers who perform 8-9 continuous dances which are a combination of Garba, dandiya-raas, and tran tali.

steps.action: All dancers are required to be college students
steps.text: All dancers are required to be college students but may attend a different college from which they represent.

steps.action: Teams are expected to be in traditional Garba-Ra
steps.text: Teams are expected to be in traditional Garba-Raas clothing.

steps.action: Routines are commonly constructed with a theme p
steps.text: Routines are commonly constructed with a theme presented throughout the dance through props and special effects.

steps.action: Dancers take up an entire auditorium stage with
steps.text: Dancers take up an entire auditorium stage with different types of formations and movements throughout an approximately six-minute dance piece.

steps.action: Throughout the piece, dancers either do choreogr
steps.text: Throughout the piece, dancers either do choreography facing the audience or interact with other partners on stage for short periods of time in a reference to the traditional roots of Garba-raas.

steps.action: Unlike traditional dandiya-raas or Garba, colleg
steps.text: Unlike traditional dandiya-raas or Garba, collegiate Garba-raas do not have a set centre focal point.

steps.action: The application commonly includes an audition vi
steps.text: The application commonly includes an audition video of the team displaying a dance to three songs.
